entities.name: Transmission Developer
entities.legal_role: The party with the contractual right to suspend work under the EPC Agreement and responsible for costs arising from suspension.
entities.evidence_quote: Transmission Developer reserves the right, upon written notice to Affected System Operator and NYISO, to suspend at any time all work by Affected System Operator associated with the EPC Services under this Agreement

entities.name: Affected System Operator
entities.legal_role: The party performing EPC Services whose work may be suspended and who incurs costs related to suspension.
entities.evidence_quote: Transmission Developer reserves the right, upon written notice to Affected System Operator and NYISO, to suspend at any time all work by Affected System Operator associated with the EPC Services under this Agreement

entities.name: NYISO
entities.legal_role: The entity with rights to require suspension of work upon termination of the Development Agreement and recipient of notices regarding suspension.
entities.evidence_quote: NYISO reserves the right, upon written notice to Transmission Developer and Affected System Operator, to require the suspension of all work by Transmission Developer and Affected System Operator associated with the EPC Services under this Agreement if the NYISO terminates the Development Agreement pursuant to Article 8 of the Development Agreement.

entities.name: EPC Services
entities.legal_role: The specific work performed by Affected System Operator under the Agreement that may be suspended.
entities.evidence_quote: to suspend at any time all work by Affected System Operator associated with the EPC Services under this Agreement

entities.name: Development Agreement
entities.legal_role: A related agreement containing milestones and termination provisions that affect suspension rights under this Agreement.
entities.evidence_quote: If the suspension will impact the Transmission Developer’s ability to meet any Advisory Milestones or Critical Path Milestones in the Development Agreement, Transmission Developer shall notify the NYISO in accordance with the requirements in Article 3.3 of the Development Agreement.

entities.name: Advisory Milestones
entities.legal_role: Milestones in the Development Agreement whose potential impact triggers notification obligations upon suspension.
entities.evidence_quote: If the suspension will impact the Transmission Developer’s ability to meet any Advisory Milestones or Critical Path Milestones in the Development Agreement, Transmission Developer shall notify the NYISO

entities.name: Critical Path Milestones
entities.legal_role: Milestones in the Development Agreement whose potential impact triggers notification obligations upon suspension.
entities.evidence_quote: If the suspension will impact the Transmission Developer’s ability to meet any Advisory Milestones or Critical Path Milestones in the Development Agreement, Transmission Developer shall notify the NYISO

entities.name: Facilities Study report
entities.legal_role: Document governing the reasonable and necessary costs and obligations for which Transmission Developer is responsible upon suspension.
entities.evidence_quote: Transmission Developer shall be responsible for all reasonable and necessary costs and/or obligations in accordance with the Facilities Study report

entities.name: Written Notice of Suspension
entities.legal_role: Formal written communication triggering suspension rights and defining the suspension commencement date.
entities.evidence_quote: Transmission Developer reserves the right, upon written notice to Affected System Operator and NYISO, to suspend at any time all work

entities.name: Three (3) year suspension period
entities.legal_role: The period after which, if work is not recommenced, the Agreement is deemed terminated.
entities.evidence_quote: if Transmission Developer suspends work ... and has not requested Affected System Operator to recommence the work ... on or before the expiration of three (3) years following commencement of such suspension, this Agreement shall be deemed terminated.

relationships.legal_significance: Transmission Developer has the contractual right to suspend work performed by Affected System Operator and is responsible for costs incurred due to suspension.
relationships.evidence_quote: Transmission Developer reserves the right ... to suspend at any time all work by Affected System Operator ... Transmission Developer shall be responsible for all reasonable and necessary costs ... including those which Affected System Operator ... incurs in suspending such work

relationships.legal_significance: Transmission Developer must notify NYISO if suspension impacts milestones in the Development Agreement.
relationships.evidence_quote: If the suspension will impact the Transmission Developer’s ability to meet any Advisory Milestones or Critical Path Milestones in the Development Agreement, Transmission Developer shall notify the NYISO

relationships.legal_significance: NYISO may require suspension of work by Transmission Developer and Affected System Operator if NYISO terminates the Development Agreement.
relationships.evidence_quote: NYISO reserves the right ... to require the suspension of all work ... if the NYISO terminates the Development Agreement pursuant to Article 8

relationships.legal_significance: Affected System Operator invoices Transmission Developer for suspension-related costs and must obtain authorization before canceling contracts.
relationships.evidence_quote: Affected System Operator shall invoice Transmission Developer for such costs ... prior to canceling or suspending any such material, equipment or labor contract, Affected System Operator shall obtain Transmission Developer’s authorization

relationships.legal_significance: The written notice of suspension starts the three-year period after which the Agreement is deemed terminated if work is not recommenced.
relationships.evidence_quote: The three-year period shall begin on the date required under this Article 3.10 or the date specified in the written notice of suspension.

extractor_notes: This clause establishes mutual suspension rights and obligations between Transmission Developer, Affected System Operator, and NYISO, linking suspension to milestone impacts and Development Agreement termination, with detailed cost responsibility and a three-year termination trigger if suspension persists.

entities.name: Indemnified Party
entities.legal_role: The party entitled to receive indemnity and who must notify the Indemnifying Party upon receipt of a claim or legal proceeding to which indemnity may apply.
entities.evidence_quote: Promptly after receipt by an Indemnified Party of any claim or notice ... the Indemnified Party shall notify the Indemnifying Party of such fact.

entities.name: Indemnifying Party
entities.legal_role: The party obligated to indemnify the Indemnified Party and who has the right to assume the defense of claims subject to indemnity.
entities.evidence_quote: the Indemnifying Party shall have the right to assume the defense thereof with counsel designated by such Indemnifying Party

entities.entity_type: Defense Counsel Designated by Indemnifying Party
entities.name: Counsel designated by Indemnifying Party
entities.legal_role: The legal counsel selected by the Indemnifying Party to defend claims subject to indemnity, subject to reasonableness approval by the Indemnified Party.
entities.evidence_quote: the Indemnifying Party shall have the right to assume the defense thereof with counsel designated by such Indemnifying Party and reasonably satisfactory to the Indemnified Party.

entities.name: Separate counsel selected by Indemnified Party
entities.legal_role: Counsel selected by the Indemnified Party to assert legal defenses different or additional to those of the Indemnifying Party, with fees partially borne by the Indemnifying Party.
entities.evidence_quote: the Indemnified Party shall have the right to select separate counsel to assert such legal defenses and to otherwise participate in the defense of such action on its own behalf.

entities.name: Claim or legal proceeding subject to indemnity
entities.legal_role: Any claim, action, administrative or legal proceeding or investigation to which the indemnity obligation applies.
entities.evidence_quote: any claim or notice of the commencement of any action or administrative or legal proceeding or investigation as to which the indemnity provided for in Article 12.1 may apply

entities.name: Indemnity obligation under Article 12.1
entities.legal_role: The contractual duty of the Indemnifying Party to indemnify the Indemnified Party for claims as specified in Article 12.1.
entities.evidence_quote: the indemnity provided for in Article 12.1 may apply

entities.name: Notification of claim to Indemnifying Party
entities.legal_role: The requirement that the Indemnified Party promptly notify the Indemnifying Party of claims or proceedings to which indemnity may apply, with limited effect of failure or delay.
entities.evidence_quote: the Indemnified Party shall notify the Indemnifying Party of such fact. Any failure of or delay in such notification shall not affect ... unless such failure or delay is materially prejudicial to the Indemnifying Party.

entities.name: Indemnifying Party's right to assume defense
entities.legal_role: The contractual right of the Indemnifying Party to control the defense of claims subject to indemnity, subject to exceptions.
entities.evidence_quote: the Indemnifying Party shall have the right to assume the defense thereof with counsel designated by such Indemnifying Party

entities.name: Indemnified Party's right to select separate counsel
entities.legal_role: The right of the Indemnified Party to select separate counsel when legal defenses differ or are additional to those of the Indemnifying Party.
entities.evidence_quote: the Indemnified Party shall have the right to select separate counsel to assert such legal defenses and to otherwise participate in the defense of such action on its own behalf.

entities.entity_type: Limit on Fees for Separate Counsel
entities.name: Indemnifying Party's obligation to pay fees for one additional attorney
entities.legal_role: The limitation that the Indemnifying Party is only required to pay fees and expenses for one additional attorney representing Indemnified Parties with differing or additional defenses.
entities.evidence_quote: the Indemnifying Party shall only be required to pay the fees and expenses of one additional attorney to represent an Indemnified Party or Indemnified Parties having such differing or additional legal defenses.

entities.entity_type: Right to Participate at Own Expense
entities.name: Indemnified Party's right to participate in defense
entities.legal_role: The right of the Indemnified Party to participate in the defense of claims assumed by the Indemnifying Party, but at its own expense.
entities.evidence_quote: The Indemnified Party shall be entitled, at its expense, to participate in any such action, suit or proceeding, the defense of which has been assumed by the Indemnifying Party.

entities.entity_type: Exception to Right to Assume Defense
entities.name: Exceptions preventing Indemnifying Party from assuming defense
entities.legal_role: Situations where the Indemnifying Party cannot assume or control defense due to potential criminal liability or conflict of interest, triggering payment of reasonable expenses by Indemnifying Party.
entities.evidence_quote: the Indemnifying Party (i) shall not be entitled to assume and control the defense ... if ... such action ... involves the potential imposition of criminal liability on the Indemnified Party, or there exists a conflict or adversity of interest between the Indemnified Party and the Indemnifying Party

entities.name: Indemnifying Party's obligation to pay reasonable expenses
entities.legal_role: The obligation of the Indemnifying Party to pay reasonable expenses of the Indemnified Party when the Indemnifying Party cannot assume or control the defense due to exceptions.
entities.evidence_quote: in such event the Indemnifying Party shall pay the reasonable expenses of the Indemnified Party

entities.name: Indemnified Party's consent to settlement
entities.legal_role: The requirement that the Indemnifying Party must obtain the Indemnified Party's consent before settling or consenting to judgment, with consent not to be unreasonably withheld.
entities.evidence_quote: the Indemnifying Party shall not settle or consent to the entry of any judgment ... without the consent of the Indemnified Party, which shall not be unreasonably withheld, conditioned or delayed.

relationships.legal_significance: The Indemnified Party must notify the Indemnifying Party promptly of claims or proceedings to which indemnity may apply, enabling the Indemnifying Party to exercise defense rights.
relationships.evidence_quote: the Indemnified Party shall notify the Indemnifying Party of such fact.

relationships.legal_significance: The Indemnifying Party has the right to select counsel to defend claims subject to indemnity, controlling the defense process.
relationships.evidence_quote: the Indemnifying Party shall have the right to assume the defense thereof with counsel designated by such Indemnifying Party

relationships.legal_significance: The Indemnified Party may select separate counsel when legal defenses differ or are additional, ensuring protection of its distinct legal interests.
relationships.evidence_quote: the Indemnified Party shall have the right to select separate counsel to assert such legal defenses

relationships.relationship_type: Limitation on Indemnifying Party's Payment for Separate Counsel
relationships.legal_significance: Limits the Indemnifying Party's financial exposure by capping payment obligations for additional counsel to one attorney despite multiple Indemnified Parties.
relationships.evidence_quote: the Indemnifying Party shall only be required to pay the fees and expenses of one additional attorney

relationships.legal_significance: Allows the Indemnified Party to be involved in the defense of claims assumed by the Indemnifying Party, preserving its interests at its own cost.
relationships.evidence_quote: The Indemnified Party shall be entitled, at its expense, to participate in any such action

relationships.legal_significance: Restricts the Indemnifying Party's right to control defense when criminal liability or conflicts of interest arise, protecting the Indemnified Party's legal rights.
relationships.evidence_quote: the Indemnifying Party shall not be entitled to assume and control the defense ... if ... potential imposition of criminal liability ... or conflict or adversity of interest

relationships.relationship_type: Obligation to Pay Expenses When Defense Control Denied
relationships.legal_significance: Requires the Indemnifying Party to pay reasonable expenses of the Indemnified Party when it cannot control the defense due to exceptions, ensuring fair cost allocation.
relationships.evidence_quote: in such event the Indemnifying Party shall pay the reasonable expenses of the Indemnified Party

relationships.legal_significance: Prevents the Indemnifying Party from settling claims without the Indemnified Party's consent, protecting the Indemnified Party's interests in resolution decisions.
relationships.evidence_quote: shall not settle or consent to the entry of any judgment ... without the consent of the Indemnified Party

extractor_notes: The clause carefully balances the rights and obligations of the Indemnified and Indemnifying Parties regarding notification, defense control, counsel selection, expense payment, and settlement consent, with explicit exceptions for criminal liability and conflicts of interest.

source_clause_title: 3.11.1 Indemnification for the Cost Consequences of Current Tax Liability Imposed

entities.name: Transmission Developer
entities.legal_role: The party obligated to protect, indemnify, hold harmless, and reimburse the Affected System Operator for certain tax liabilities and related costs under this Agreement.
entities.evidence_quote: Transmission Developer shall protect, indemnify and hold harmless Affected System Operator from the cost consequences of any current tax liability imposed against Affected System Operator...

entities.name: Affected System Operator
entities.legal_role: The party entitled to protection, indemnification, and reimbursement from the Transmission Developer for current tax liabilities and related costs arising from payments or property transfers under this Agreement.
entities.evidence_quote: Transmission Developer shall protect, indemnify and hold harmless Affected System Operator from the cost consequences of any current tax liability imposed against Affected System Operator...

entities.name: Current tax liability imposed against Affected System Operator
entities.legal_role: A financial obligation imposed on the Affected System Operator as a result of payments or property transfers made by the Transmission Developer under this Agreement, triggering indemnification.
entities.evidence_quote: any current tax liability imposed against Affected System Operator as the result of payments or property transfers made by Transmission Developer to Affected System Operator under this Agreement

entities.name: Interest and penalties related to current tax liability
entities.legal_role: Additional financial charges related to the current tax liability for which the Transmission Developer must indemnify the Affected System Operator, except those caused by the Operator's delay.
entities.evidence_quote: as well as any interest and penalties, other than interest and penalties attributable to any delay caused by Affected System Operator

entities.name: Reimbursement of costs on a fully grossed-up basis
entities.legal_role: The Transmission Developer's obligation to reimburse the Affected System Operator for indemnified costs, calculated and paid within thirty calendar days upon written notification.
entities.evidence_quote: Transmission Developer shall reimburse Affected System Operator for such costs on a fully grossed-up basis, in accordance with Article 2, within thirty (30) Calendar Days of receiving written notification

entities.name: Termination of indemnification obligation
entities.legal_role: The conditions under which the Transmission Developer's indemnification obligation ends, either after the ten-year testing period and statute of limitations or upon a subsequent taxable event and related indemnification payment.
entities.evidence_quote: This indemnification obligation shall terminate at the earlier of (1) the expiration of the ten-year testing period and the applicable statute of limitation... or (2) the occurrence of a subsequent taxable event and the payment of any related indemnification obligations

entities.name: Extension of statute of limitations by Affected System Operator upon IRS request
entities.legal_role: The Affected System Operator's right to extend the statute of limitations period to keep the tax years open for audit or adjustment, affecting the duration of indemnification.
entities.evidence_quote: as it may be extended by the Affected System Operator upon request of the IRS, to keep these years open for audit or adjustment

relationships.legal_significance: The Transmission Developer is legally required to indemnify and hold harmless the Affected System Operator against certain tax liabilities and related costs, protecting the Operator from financial harm.
relationships.evidence_quote: Transmission Developer shall protect, indemnify and hold harmless Affected System Operator from the cost consequences of any current tax liability imposed against Affected System Operator

relationships.legal_significance: The current tax liability imposed on the Affected System Operator as a result of payments or property transfers by the Transmission Developer triggers the indemnification obligation.
relationships.evidence_quote: any current tax liability imposed against Affected System Operator as the result of payments or property transfers made by Transmission Developer

relationships.legal_significance: The Transmission Developer must indemnify interest and penalties related to the tax liability except those caused by delay attributable to the Affected System Operator, defining the scope of indemnification.
relationships.evidence_quote: as well as any interest and penalties, other than interest and penalties attributable to any delay caused by Affected System Operator

relationships.legal_significance: The Transmission Developer must reimburse the Affected System Operator for indemnified costs on a fully grossed-up basis within a specified timeframe, ensuring timely compensation.
relationships.evidence_quote: Transmission Developer shall reimburse Affected System Operator for such costs on a fully grossed-up basis... within thirty (30) Calendar Days of receiving written notification

relationships.legal_significance: Defines when the Transmission Developer's indemnification obligation ceases, limiting the duration of liability and providing certainty to the parties.
relationships.evidence_quote: This indemnification obligation shall terminate at the earlier of (1) the expiration of the ten-year testing period and the applicable statute of limitation... or (2) the occurrence of a subsequent taxable event and the payment of any related indemnification obligations

relationships.legal_significance: The Affected System Operator's ability to extend the statute of limitations period upon IRS request prolongs the indemnification obligation, affecting risk exposure.
relationships.evidence_quote: as it may be extended by the Affected System Operator upon request of the IRS, to keep these years open for audit or adjustment

extractor_notes: The clause explicitly limits indemnification to current tax liabilities arising from payments or property transfers under the Agreement and excludes penalties caused by the Operator's delay. The indemnification period is tied to a ten-year testing period and statute of limitations, which may be extended by IRS request, or terminated upon a subsequent taxable event. The reimbursement is on a fully grossed-up basis, ensuring the Operator is made whole.

entities.name: Security
entities.legal_role: The Security represents a financial guarantee provided by the Transmission Developer to secure performance obligations and is subject to forfeiture upon certain termination or abandonment events.
entities.evidence_quote: The Security the Transmission Developer provides Affected System Operator in accordance with Article 6.2 of this Agreement shall be irrevocable and shall be subject to forfeiture...

entities.name: Transmission Developer
entities.legal_role: The party responsible for developing the Transmission Project and providing Security to the Affected System Operator under the Agreement.
entities.evidence_quote: The Security the Transmission Developer provides Affected System Operator...

entities.name: Affected System Operator
entities.legal_role: The party receiving the Security from the Transmission Developer and entitled to enforce forfeiture under specified conditions.
entities.evidence_quote: The Security the Transmission Developer provides Affected System Operator...

entities.name: Transmission Project
entities.legal_role: The project being developed by the Transmission Developer, whose termination or abandonment triggers potential forfeiture of Security.
entities.evidence_quote: in the event that the Transmission Developer subsequently terminates or abandons development of the Transmission Project...

entities.name: Affected System Upgrade Facilities
entities.legal_role: Facilities related to the Transmission Project whose development abandonment or termination can cause forfeiture of Security.
entities.evidence_quote: terminates or abandons development of the Transmission Project or the Affected System Upgrade Facilities.

entities.name: Other Transmission Developers
entities.legal_role: Third-party developers whose required Affected System Upgrade Facilities costs may be defrayed by forfeited Security.
entities.evidence_quote: (1) Affected System Upgrade Facilities required for other Transmission Developers whose Transmission Project interconnection studies included the Transmission Developer’s Transmission Project...

entities.name: System Upgrade Facilities
entities.legal_role: Facilities required for projects that may be funded by forfeited Security when the Transmission Project and related facilities are included in certain studies.
entities.evidence_quote: (2) System Upgrade Facilities and System Deliverability Upgrade Facilities required for projects for which the Transmission Project and Affected System Upgrade Facilities were included in their Annual Transmission Reliability Assessment and/or Class Year Deliverability Study...

entities.name: System Deliverability Upgrade Facilities
entities.legal_role: Specific upgrade facilities whose costs may be defrayed by forfeited Security under the conditions described.
entities.evidence_quote: (2) System Upgrade Facilities and System Deliverability Upgrade Facilities required for projects...

entities.name: Connecting Transmission Owner
entities.legal_role: The party entitled to draw on the Security to defray costs of affected upgrades and negotiate replacement Security forms with the Transmission Developer.
entities.evidence_quote: Transmission Developer shall negotiate in good faith with the Connecting Transmission Owner to replace the Security with cash or an alternative form of Security... Connecting Transmission Owner shall only be responsible for using Transmission Developer’s Security to defray the costs...

relationships.legal_significance: The Transmission Developer is legally obligated to provide irrevocable Security to secure its performance under the Agreement.
relationships.evidence_quote: The Security the Transmission Developer provides Affected System Operator in accordance with Article 6.2 of this Agreement...

relationships.legal_significance: The Security is held by the Affected System Operator, who has the right to enforce forfeiture upon certain triggering events.
relationships.evidence_quote: The Security the Transmission Developer provides Affected System Operator...

relationships.legal_significance: The Transmission Developer is responsible for developing the Transmission Project, whose termination or abandonment triggers forfeiture of Security.
relationships.evidence_quote: in the event that the Transmission Developer subsequently terminates or abandons development of the Transmission Project...

relationships.legal_significance: The Transmission Developer is responsible for developing the Affected System Upgrade Facilities, whose abandonment or termination triggers forfeiture of Security.
relationships.evidence_quote: terminates or abandons development of the Transmission Project or the Affected System Upgrade Facilities.

relationships.legal_significance: The Security may be forfeited to defray costs of Affected System Upgrade Facilities required for other Transmission Developers impacted by the Transmission Developer’s project.
relationships.evidence_quote: Any Security provided by the Transmission Developer shall be subject to forfeiture to the extent necessary to defray the cost of: (1) Affected System Upgrade Facilities required for other Transmission Developers...

relationships.legal_significance: The Security may be forfeited to defray costs of System Upgrade Facilities required for projects including the Transmission Developer’s project in certain studies.
relationships.evidence_quote: (2) System Upgrade Facilities and System Deliverability Upgrade Facilities required for projects for which the Transmission Project and Affected System Upgrade Facilities were included in their Annual Transmission Reliability Assessment and/or Class Year Deliverability Study...

relationships.legal_significance: The Security may be forfeited to defray costs of System Deliverability Upgrade Facilities required for projects including the Transmission Developer’s project in certain studies.
relationships.evidence_quote: (2) System Upgrade Facilities and System Deliverability Upgrade Facilities required for projects...

relationships.legal_significance: If the Security is not readily drawable by the Connecting Transmission Owner, the Transmission Developer must negotiate in good faith to replace it with cash or an alternative readily drawable Security.
relationships.evidence_quote: Transmission Developer shall negotiate in good faith with the Connecting Transmission Owner to replace the Security with cash or an alternative form of Security that can be readily drawn on...

relationships.legal_significance: The Connecting Transmission Owner has the right to draw on the Security to defray costs of affected upgrades, but only if the Security is in a form readily drawable by them.
relationships.evidence_quote: Connecting Transmission Owner shall only be responsible for using Transmission Developer’s Security to defray the costs of an affected upgrade to the extent Transmission Developer has provided cash or Security in a form that the Connecting Transmission Owner can readily draw on...

extractor_notes: The clause explicitly defines the conditions under which the Transmission Developer's Security is forfeited, the parties entitled to hold and draw on the Security, and the obligation to negotiate replacement Security forms if the original Security is not readily drawable by the Connecting Transmission Owner.

entities.name: Transmission Developer
entities.legal_role: The Transmission Developer is a party entitled to receive payments and obligated to submit invoices under the Agreement.
entities.evidence_quote: any amounts are due to the Transmission Developer ... the Transmission Developer ... shall submit ... invoices

entities.name: Affected System Operator
entities.legal_role: The Affected System Operator is a party entitled to receive payments and obligated to submit invoices under the Agreement.
entities.evidence_quote: any amounts are due to ... Affected System Operator ... Affected System Operator ... shall submit ... invoices

entities.name: Invoice
entities.legal_role: An invoice is a formal document submitted monthly by either party to request payment for amounts due under the Agreement.
entities.evidence_quote: shall submit to the other Party, on a monthly basis, invoices of amounts due for the preceding month

entities.name: EPC Services
entities.legal_role: EPC Services are the specific services performed under the Agreement for which final payment invoices must be submitted within six months after completion.
entities.evidence_quote: Within six months after completion of the EPC Services ... shall provide a final invoice

entities.name: Mutual Debts and Payment Obligations
entities.legal_role: Mutual debts and payment obligations represent reciprocal amounts owed by the parties to each other that may be netted to simplify payment.
entities.evidence_quote: may discharge mutual debts and payment obligations due and owing to each other on the same date through netting

relationships.legal_significance: Each party is obligated to submit invoices to the other party for amounts due, establishing a reciprocal invoicing process.
relationships.evidence_quote: the Transmission Developer or Affected System Operator ... shall submit to the other Party ... invoices

relationships.legal_significance: The Transmission Developer issues invoices to claim payment for services or equipment provided under the Agreement.
relationships.evidence_quote: the Transmission Developer ... shall submit ... invoices of amounts due

relationships.legal_significance: The Affected System Operator issues invoices to claim payment for services or equipment provided under the Agreement.
relationships.evidence_quote: Affected System Operator ... shall submit ... invoices of amounts due

relationships.legal_significance: The Transmission Developer participates in netting mutual debts and payment obligations to reduce payment complexity and amount owed.
relationships.evidence_quote: The Transmission Developer ... may discharge mutual debts and payment obligations ... through netting

relationships.legal_significance: The Affected System Operator participates in netting mutual debts and payment obligations to reduce payment complexity and amount owed.
relationships.evidence_quote: Affected System Operator ... may discharge mutual debts and payment obligations ... through netting

relationships.legal_significance: A final invoice must be submitted within six months after completion of the EPC Services to settle any remaining amounts due.
relationships.evidence_quote: Within six months after completion of the EPC Services ... shall provide a final invoice

extractor_notes: The clause establishes reciprocal invoicing obligations between the Transmission Developer and Affected System Operator, including monthly invoicing, detailed invoice content requirements, mutual debt netting to simplify payments, and a final invoice submission deadline tied to completion of EPC Services.

source_clause_title: 16.12 Disclosure to FERC, its Staff, or a State.

entities.name: FERC
entities.legal_role: FERC is the federal regulatory authority empowered to request confidential information from the Parties during investigations or otherwise under 18 C.F.R. regulations.
entities.evidence_quote: if FERC or its staff, during the course of an investigation or otherwise, requests information from one of the Parties

entities.name: FERC staff
entities.legal_role: FERC staff act as agents of FERC authorized to request and receive confidential information from the Parties under regulatory authority.
entities.evidence_quote: if FERC or its staff, during the course of an investigation or otherwise, requests information

entities.name: Party
entities.legal_role: A Party is a signatory to the Agreement obligated to maintain confidentiality but required to provide requested information to FERC or its staff under regulatory mandate.
entities.evidence_quote: if FERC or its staff ... requests information from one of the Parties ... the Party shall provide the requested information

entities.name: Confidential Information
entities.legal_role: Information required to be maintained in confidence under the Agreement or ISO OATT, subject to disclosure only under specified regulatory conditions.
entities.evidence_quote: information from one of the Parties that is otherwise required to be maintained in confidence pursuant to this Agreement or the ISO OATT

entities.name: ISO OATT
entities.legal_role: The ISO Open Access Transmission Tariff (OATT) is a regulatory document that imposes confidentiality obligations on the Parties.
entities.evidence_quote: information ... required to be maintained in confidence pursuant to this Agreement or the ISO OATT

entities.name: 18 C.F.R. section 1b.20
entities.legal_role: Federal regulation authorizing FERC or its staff to request confidential information from the Parties during investigations or otherwise.
entities.evidence_quote: pursuant to 18 C.F.R. section 1b.20, if FERC or its staff ... requests information

entities.name: 18 C.F.R. section 388.112
entities.legal_role: Federal regulation requiring the Party providing information to request confidential and non-public treatment of such information by FERC and its staff.
entities.evidence_quote: consistent with 18 C.F.R. section 388.112, request that the information be treated as confidential and non-public

entities.name: Other Parties to this Agreement
entities.legal_role: Parties to the Agreement other than the disclosing Party, who are prohibited from being notified prior to release of Confidential Information to FERC or its staff.
entities.evidence_quote: Parties are prohibited from notifying the other Parties to this Agreement prior to the release of the Confidential Information

entities.name: Article 16
entities.legal_role: The confidentiality provisions of the Agreement which are subject to exception under this clause for disclosures to FERC or its staff.
entities.evidence_quote: Notwithstanding anything in this Article 16 to the contrary

relationships.legal_significance: The Party is legally obligated to provide requested confidential information to FERC or its staff within the time specified in the request, overriding confidentiality provisions.
relationships.evidence_quote: the Party shall provide the requested information to FERC or its staff, within the time provided for in the request for information

relationships.legal_significance: The Party must maintain the confidentiality of certain information pursuant to the Agreement and ISO OATT, except as required by regulatory disclosure.
relationships.evidence_quote: information from one of the Parties that is otherwise required to be maintained in confidence pursuant to this Agreement or the ISO OATT

relationships.legal_significance: The Party must comply with 18 C.F.R. section 388.112 by requesting confidential and non-public treatment of information provided to FERC or its staff.
relationships.evidence_quote: In providing the information to FERC or its staff, the Party must, consistent with 18 C.F.R. section 388.112, request that the information be treated as confidential and non-public

relationships.legal_significance: The Party is prohibited from notifying the other Parties prior to release of Confidential Information to FERC or its staff, preserving regulatory confidentiality.
relationships.evidence_quote: Parties are prohibited from notifying the other Parties to this Agreement prior to the release of the Confidential Information

relationships.legal_significance: FERC staff have the authority to request Confidential Information from the Parties during investigations or otherwise.
relationships.evidence_quote: if FERC or its staff ... requests information from one of the Parties

relationships.legal_significance: FERC has the authority to request Confidential Information from the Parties during investigations or otherwise.
relationships.evidence_quote: if FERC or its staff ... requests information from one of the Parties

relationships.relationship_type: Notification Obligation Upon FERC Request to Release
relationships.legal_significance: The Party must notify the other Parties when it is notified by FERC or its staff of a request to release Confidential Information, allowing the Parties to respond before public disclosure.
relationships.evidence_quote: The Party shall notify the other Parties to the Agreement when it is notified by FERC or its staff that a request to release Confidential Information has been received by FERC

relationships.legal_significance: This clause overrides the confidentiality obligations in Article 16 to permit disclosure to FERC or its staff as required by federal regulation.
relationships.evidence_quote: Notwithstanding anything in this Article 16 to the contrary, and pursuant to 18 C.F.R. section 1b.20, if FERC or its staff ... requests information ... the Party shall provide the requested information

extractor_notes: The clause explicitly creates an exception to the confidentiality obligations in Article 16 for disclosures to FERC or its staff under federal regulations 18 C.F.R. sections 1b.20 and 388.112, including procedural requirements for confidentiality requests and notification restrictions among Parties.

entities.name: This Agreement

entities.name: Agreement

entities.evidence_quote: necessary to fulfill its obligations under this Agreement

relationships.legal_significance: A Party possesses Confidential Information and is responsible for its protection and controlled disclosure.
relationships.evidence_quote: no Party shall disclose Confidential Information to any person not employed or retained by the Party possessing the Confidential Information

relationships.legal_significance: The Disclosing Party must obtain the Other Party’s consent, which cannot be unreasonably withheld, before disclosing Confidential Information outside permitted exceptions.
relationships.evidence_quote: otherwise permitted by consent of the other Party, such consent not to be unreasonably withheld

relationships.relationship_type: Must Notify Upon Third Party or Governmental Request

relationships.legal_significance: The Disclosing Party may disclose Confidential Information without breaching confidentiality if disclosure is required by law, reasonably required for dispute resolution or litigation defense, permitted by consent, or necessary to fulfill obligations under the Agreement, ISO OATT, or NYISO Services Tariff.

entities.name: standard safety practices
entities.legal_role: The safety standards that the Affected System Operator must not violate in performing the EPC Services.
entities.evidence_quote: shall not be required to undertake any action which is inconsistent with its standard safety practices

entities.name: material and equipment specifications
entities.legal_role: The technical standards governing materials and equipment that the Affected System Operator must adhere to.
entities.evidence_quote: shall not be required to undertake any action which is inconsistent with ... its material and equipment specifications

entities.name: design criteria and construction procedures
entities.legal_role: The design and construction standards that limit the actions the Affected System Operator may take.
entities.evidence_quote: shall not be required to undertake any action which is inconsistent with ... its design criteria and construction procedures

entities.name: labor agreements
entities.legal_role: The labor contracts that constrain the Affected System Operator's permissible actions in performing the EPC Services.
entities.evidence_quote: shall not be required to undertake any action which is inconsistent with ... its labor agreements

entities.name: Applicable Laws and Regulations
entities.legal_role: The legal framework that the Affected System Operator must comply with in performing the EPC Services.
entities.evidence_quote: shall not be required to undertake any action which is inconsistent with ... Applicable Laws and Regulations

entities.name: Transmission Developer
entities.legal_role: A party entitled to receive notice if the Affected System Operator expects delay in EPC Services completion.
entities.evidence_quote: the Affected System Operator shall promptly provide written notice to the Transmission Developer and NYISO

entities.entity_type: Contract Counterparty and Liability Exempted Party
entities.name: NYISO
entities.legal_role: A party entitled to receive notice of delays and expressly exempted from liability for EPC Services performance.
entities.evidence_quote: the Affected System Operator shall promptly provide written notice to the Transmission Developer and NYISO ... The NYISO has no responsibility, and shall have no liability, for the performance of any of the EPC Services under this Agreement

relationships.legal_significance: The Affected System Operator is contractually obligated to perform the EPC Services, defining the primary duty under this clause.
relationships.evidence_quote: The Affected System Operator shall perform the EPC Services, as set forth in Appendix A hereto

relationships.legal_significance: The EPC Services must be completed by the Milestone dates, establishing enforceable timeframes.
relationships.evidence_quote: using Reasonable Efforts to complete the EPC Services by the Milestone dates set forth in Appendix A hereto

relationships.legal_significance: The Affected System Operator must apply Reasonable Efforts, setting the standard of care for performance.
relationships.evidence_quote: using Reasonable Efforts to complete the EPC Services by the Milestone dates

relationships.legal_significance: The Affected System Operator is not required to act inconsistently with its standard safety practices, limiting its obligations.
relationships.evidence_quote: shall not be required to undertake any action which is inconsistent with its standard safety practices

relationships.legal_significance: The Affected System Operator's actions are limited by its material and equipment specifications.
relationships.evidence_quote: shall not be required to undertake any action which is inconsistent with ... its material and equipment specifications

relationships.legal_significance: The Affected System Operator must comply with its design criteria and construction procedures, restricting its actions.
relationships.evidence_quote: shall not be required to undertake any action which is inconsistent with ... its design criteria and construction procedures

relationships.legal_significance: The Affected System Operator's performance is constrained by its labor agreements.
relationships.evidence_quote: shall not be required to undertake any action which is inconsistent with ... its labor agreements

relationships.legal_significance: The Affected System Operator must comply with Applicable Laws and Regulations in performing the EPC Services.
relationships.evidence_quote: shall not be required to undertake any action which is inconsistent with ... Applicable Laws and Regulations

relationships.legal_significance: The Affected System Operator must provide prompt written notice to the Transmission Developer if it expects delay, enabling risk management.
relationships.evidence_quote: the Affected System Operator shall promptly provide written notice to the Transmission Developer and NYISO

relationships.legal_significance: The Affected System Operator must provide prompt written notice to NYISO if it expects delay, ensuring transparency.
relationships.evidence_quote: the Affected System Operator shall promptly provide written notice to the Transmission Developer and NYISO

relationships.legal_significance: NYISO disclaims responsibility and liability for the performance of the EPC Services, limiting its legal exposure.
relationships.evidence_quote: The NYISO has no responsibility, and shall have no liability, for the performance of any of the EPC Services under this Agreement

extractor_notes: The clause explicitly limits the Affected System Operator's obligations by multiple compliance and safety standards and exempts NYISO from liability, which is critical for risk allocation.
